title: Ines and the Big Bad Thunderstorm
synopsis: When a loud thunderstorm frightens little Ines, she learns that being scared is okay and discovers ways to feel brave with help from her loving family.

pages.text: Ines was playing with her favorite dolls when she heard a big BOOM! The thunder made her jump. Her dark eyes grew wide and her heart beat fast. "Mami!" she called out, feeling very scared. The loud noise made her want to hide under her colorful blanket.

illustration.description: A young Latina girl with curly black hair and big dark eyes sits on a bright yellow rug, surrounded by dolls of different ethnicities. She looks startled with wide eyes and slightly open mouth. Her bedroom has cheerful primary colors - red bookshelf, blue toy box, yellow walls. Lightning flashes outside her window, casting dramatic shadows. The style is bright cartoon with rounded, friendly shapes and slightly textured backgrounds.
illustration.alt: Scared little girl Ines in her colorful bedroom during a thunderstorm

pages.text: Mami rushed into the room with open arms. She had warm brown skin like Ines and gentle eyes full of love. "Mi niña, are you scared of the thunder?" she asked softly. Ines nodded and ran to hug her tight. "It's so loud, Mami! It makes me feel scared inside," Ines whispered into her mother's soft sweater.

illustration.description: A loving Latina mother with shoulder-length black hair and warm brown skin kneels down with arms outstretched toward Ines. Both wear bright, cheerful clothing - Mami in a coral sweater and Ines in a purple dress with flower patterns. The room is warmly lit despite the storm outside. Their expressions show love and comfort, with soft, rounded features typical of friendly cartoon style.
illustration.alt: Mami comforting scared Ines with a warm hug

pages.text: "Being scared is okay, mi amor," Mami said, stroking Ines's curly hair. "But let's find ways to feel brave together." Just then, Papi appeared in the doorway. He was tall with kind eyes behind his glasses and a big smile. "Did someone say brave?" he asked with a wink. Ines felt a little better seeing both her parents there.

illustration.description: The family scene shows Mami holding Ines while Papi stands in the doorway. Papi is a Latino man with short black hair, glasses, and a warm smile, wearing a green button-up shirt. The composition shows all three family members with the storm visible through the window but the indoor lighting warm and cozy. Bold primary colors dominate - green, coral, purple, yellow walls. Everyone has expressive, caring faces.
illustration.alt: The complete family together - Mami, Papi, and Ines - during the thunderstorm

pages.text: Papi sat down and pulled out his phone. "Let's listen to happy music together," he said. Soon, a cheerful song filled the room. Mami started dancing silly, making funny faces that made Ines giggle. When the next thunder came, they all clapped along with the beat. "See? Thunder is just sky music!" laughed Papi. Ines felt brave and happy dancing with her family.

illustration.description: All three family members are dancing joyfully in the bedroom. Mami has her arms up in a silly dance pose with a big smile, Papi is clapping with his phone playing music (musical notes float around), and Ines is giggling and starting to dance too. The room is filled with warm, bright colors and the storm outside seems less scary now. Their expressions are full of joy and the mood is celebratory and brave.
illustration.alt: The happy family dancing together, feeling brave during the storm

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Ines feel when she heard the thunder?
questions.explanation: Ines felt scared when she heard the loud thunder go BOOM!

questions.question: Who came to help Ines when she was scared?
questions.explanation: Both Mami and Papi came to help Ines feel better when she was scared.

questions.question: What did the family do to feel brave?
questions.explanation: The family danced to happy music together, which helped Ines feel brave.

questions.question: How did Ines feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: At the end, Ines felt brave and happy dancing with her family.

pages.text: Ines was playing when she heard a big ___! The thunder made her feel very ___. "Mami!" she called out, wanting to hide under her ___ blanket.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Page 2
